message.content:
<deliberation>
Step 1: Understand scope and inputs
- Main REG nodes to evaluate: REG-075 (Art45(7)), REG-069 (Art45(1)), REG-022 (Art15(2)), REG-080 (Art46(3)), REG-078 (Art46(1)).
- Cross-references available: REG-073 (Art45(5)), REG-071 (Art45(3)), REG-079 (Art46(2)), REG-081 (Art46(4)), REG-082 (Art46(5)).
- Target policy chunk describes sharing with processors and independent recipients, including transfer to PwC IT Services Ltd. in the UK, and states: “If we pass on data to service providers bound by instructions, we do not require a separate legal basis for this.”

Step 2: Summarize regulatory requirements and relevant cross-references
- REG-075 Art45(7): Commission actions under Art45(5) are without prejudice to transfers under Arts46–49. Cross-references: Art45(5) (REG-073) and Arts46(1)-(5) (REG-078-082).
- REG-069 Art45(1): Transfers permitted based on an adequacy decision; no specific authorisation required. Cross-reference: Art45(3) (REG-071) detailing adequacy decisions.
- REG-022 Art15(2): Data subjects have right to be informed of appropriate safeguards pursuant to Art46 when data are transferred to a third country/IO. Cross-references: Art46(1)-(5) (REG-078-082).
- REG-080 Art46(3): With SA authorisation, safeguards may be via contractual clauses or administrative arrangements; related consistency mechanism in 46(4) and relationship to other safeguard bases in 46(2); transitional 46(5). Cross-references: REG-078, REG-079, REG-081, REG-082.
- REG-078 Art46(1): In absence of Art45(3) adequacy, transfers only with appropriate safeguards and enforceable rights/effective remedies. Cross-references: Art45(3) (REG-071) and Art46(2)-(5) (REG-079-082).

Step 3: Assess policy chunk against each REG node (noting that omissions must be treated as Non-Deviation)
- The policy mentions transfers to the UK and other “foreign” PwC entities but does not articulate adequacy decisions (Art45) or safeguards (Art46) or the Art15(2) right. Under the instructions, mere omissions are Non-Deviation.
- Check for any positive contradictions:
  • No claim that adequacy is unnecessary or that transfers occur without safeguards where required.
  • The statement about “no separate legal basis” concerns controller–processor legal basis, not authorisations or safeguards under Arts45–46; it does not contradict any of the evaluated provisions.
  • No statements negating data subject rights or altering timelines/roles under the cited Articles.

Conclusion: For each REG node, no deviation can be evidenced from the policy text. Cross-references considered in the reasoning are listed per item.
</deliberation>
